audit_claims.claim: The admitted Springer body is 3812 bytes with SHA-256 c2429869f6fa47ab7388134b580b014efae01e6f0e474f5bab2233afb1ef6990 and has two hash-linked successful HTTPS retrieval records.
The seed has exactly the all-zero K5s [6,12,17,36,42] and [6,12,31,36,42], and no all-one K5.
Every one of the 903 one-edge flips has burden at least 2.
Every one of the 407253 distinct two-edge flips has burden at least 2, including the 403809-pair deletion-remainder-change family.
Exactly two radius-two pairs attain burden 2: {(6,12),(9,15)} and {(33,39),(36,42)}.
The two edited graphs equal the original under u -> u-27 and u -> u+27 modulo 43.
Twenty cyclic-distance orbits are constant and only distance 6 varies.
The active one-orbit constraints reduce to translated positive pairs {0,18} and {0,20}, each with multiplicity 2, and negative triple {0,5,24}, with multiplicity 1.
There are 215 active five-sets and 129 unique one-orbit constraints, with all C(43,5)=962598 five-sets accounted for.
Exactly 86 distance-six words attain burden 2: 43 rotations each of step-27 intervals of lengths 24 and 25.

new_two_orbit_result.family_definition: For each d in {1,...,21} except 6, F_{6,d} varies exactly the 43 distance-6 and 43 distance-d edges of the authenticated Springer K43 matrix and freezes the other 19 cyclic-distance orbits.
new_two_orbit_result.minimum_classification: In every family equality freezes distance d to its Springer color; the distance-6 word is one of the same 86 interval words, comprising two common-rotation and two graph-isomorphism classes.

field_progress_gate.description: Zero-conflict K43 witness passing two exact checkers
field_progress_gate.reason: No two-orbit family contains a burden-zero assignment.

field_progress_gate.description: Lower conflict count in a broader independently interesting precise family
field_progress_gate.reason: The exact minimum remains 2 in all 20 families and no new burden-two class appears.

field_progress_gate.description: Concise structural theorem explaining failure of the two-orbit or near-circulant family
field_progress_gate.reason: A precise certificate-backed finite statement was obtained, but no concise human-checkable structural proof currently explains all 20 exclusions.

field_progress_gate.description: Certified exclusions materially shrinking an accepted R(5,5) search space
field_progress_gate.reason: The encodings, proof logs, proof checks, and symmetry coverage are retained, but these 20 near-circulant slices are not an accepted material fraction of the global R(5,5) search space and have no external acceptance event.
No search-algorithm benchmark was performed in this experiment.

contribution_assessment.exact_contribution_class: verified negative experiment plus certificate-carrying research infrastructure
contribution_assessment.closest_prior_result_or_baseline: Molnar et al.'s published two-conflict K43 seed and the retained exact distance-six one-orbit minimum-two classification; pure circulants are already closed at C(5,5)=41.
contribution_assessment.measurable_improvement: Verification improved from agreement of two local enumerators to deterministic CNFs, 63 checked DRAT proofs in total, independent unsymmetrized Z3 checks for every two-orbit decision, and dual full-graph validation of all 86 one-orbit optima. Family breadth increased from one 43-variable slice to twenty precisely defined 86-variable slices. The best conflict count and Ramsey bound did not improve.
contribution_assessment.independent_validation: Line-oriented Python parser and full five-set scanner; C++20 triangle/common-neighborhood bitset evaluator; byte-for-byte radius-two ledger replay; CaDiCaL 3.0.1 proof generation; drat-trim proof checking; Z3 4.15.4 unsymmetrized pseudo-Boolean checks.
contribution_assessment.accepted_external_audience_or_channel: None. No expert, repository, venue, or peer-review process has accepted this result.
what_remains_unproved: No K43 Ramsey witness was found or excluded globally.
Global Ramsey multiplicity at order 43 is not proved to be 2.
No family releasing three or more cyclic-distance orbits is excluded.
No human-checkable theorem currently explains the two-orbit obstruction.
Historical novelty and external interest in the finite classification are not established.

route_recommendation.closed_route: Do not continue the one-orbit or two-orbit witness sweep, and do not launch all 190 three-orbit families by default.

next_experiment.name: proof-core compression for a human two-orbit obstruction theorem
next_experiment.hypothesis: The 20 unsymmetrized burden-zero exclusions admit rotation-normalized minimal raw-clause cores of at most 20 clauses that collapse to at most five parameterized obstruction types, enabling a concise human-checkable theorem.
next_experiment.discriminator: Extract deletion-minimal raw K5-clause cores for every d, canonicalize their signed incidence hypergraphs, and require a deterministic map from all 20 distances to at most five symbolic templates whose clauses can be checked directly from K43 five-sets.
next_experiment.success_certificate: A short theorem and proof, plus a script that instantiates each template for every d and verifies the originating five-set identities without SAT auxiliaries or symmetry assumptions.
next_experiment.stop_condition: Stop and abandon theorem compression if any exact core still exceeds 20 raw clauses after deletion minimization or if more than five nonisomorphic core types remain; then redirect to the leakage-safe held-out search-algorithm benchmark required by gate condition 5.
